blocks.content: Julian was on the front porch of the neighboring house when Davenport came out.
He had a mug of coffee. He was not pretending to just happen to be there — the hour made pretense impossible, and Davenport was the kind of man who would notice pretense and like you less for it.
Davenport saw him. Stopped on the path between the two houses and looked at him with the expression of a man who had been doing this long enough to know when someone was waiting for him specifically.

blocks.speaker: Davenport
blocks.content: You're in the rental next door.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Through the wall. Voices, elevated. I couldn't make out words.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: I noticed it around ten fifty. I was already awake.
Davenport looked at the mug. Then at Julian. The kind of look that takes its time.

blocks.speaker: Julian
blocks.content: Because I noticed something last night. Walking the beach. And it was the kind of thing I couldn't set down.
